title: Blue Light Glasses Don't Work. A Multi-Hundred-Million-Dollar Industry Depends on You Not Knowing That.
description: The blue light blocking glasses market has ballooned into a multi-hundred-million-dollar industry on the back of a single claim: that filtering blue wavelengths from screens will protect your sleep. The most rigorous recent science — gold-standard polysomnography trials, independent meta-analyses, the American Academy of Ophthalmology — says that claim doesn't hold up. This piece investigates how a convenient, profitable myth took root, who benefits from keeping it alive, and what the evidence actually says about screens and sleep.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Blue Light Glasses Don't Work. A Multi-Hundred-Million-Dollar Industry Depends on You Not Knowing That.
